description_long: This is a large, curved stone fragment featuring detailed ancient Egyptian-style carvings and hieroglyphics. The surface is weathered, with visible chips and erosion along the top edge, suggesting significant age and exposure. The main face displays a prominent figure in profile, likely a pharaoh or deity, with arms raised in a ritualistic gesture, surrounded by vertical and horizontal bands of hieroglyphic inscriptions. The stone is a reddish-brown color, with a rough, matte finish and granular texture typical of sandstone or similar material. The block is hollowed and incomplete, with broken, jagged edges and a cylindrical curvature, indicating it was once part of a larger architectural or monumental structure. The engravings are deep and well-defined, though some areas show signs of wear and partial loss of detail.
description: A weathered, curved stone block with ancient Egyptian carvings and hieroglyphics, reddish-brown in color and partially broken. The surface is rough and matte, with deep engravings and visible signs of age.
description_view_0: The main carved figure and most hieroglyphics are visible, with the top edge showing significant chipping and erosion.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the cylindrical curvature and vertical bands of hieroglyphics, with less detail of the main figure.
description_view_2: Frontal view showing the full carved figure, ritual gesture, and surrounding inscriptions, with the curvature and broken edges clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mostly showing the stone's curvature and rough, unadorned surface, with minimal visible carvings.
attribute_installation_method: embedded in masonry or set atop a stone foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: Chipped and eroded edges, with surface wear and some loss of carving detail, indicating long-term exposure to the elements.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: Yes, features deep ancient Egyptian-style carvings and hieroglyphic inscriptions.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: Appears to have been part of a continuous wall or monumental column.
